category_title: Ninth-grade students with learning disabilities in inclusive core academic classrooms
minimal_context: The student is expected to complete a 15-minute independent warm-up worksheet on context clues at their desk.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Alright, it's time for our 15-minute warm-up. Please get your pen out and start working on the context clues worksheet on your desk.

STUDENT: (Staring at the wall, not holding a pen) I don't want to do this right now.

TUTOR: You need to complete the worksheet.

STUDENT: (Looks at the paper but doesn't pick up the pen) These words don't even make sense. I don't know how to do context clues.

TUTOR: Just read the sentences and try to figure it out.

STUDENT: (Pushes the worksheet away, eyes and head not oriented towards the assignment) No, I'm not doing it. It's too hard and I'm not going to sit here and do this.

options.A: Provide a verbal reminder to complete the worksheet and try to walk by the student's desk more often during the independent warm-up.
options.B: Provide specific praise to the student for using context clues on the last two problems and for working and following directions.
options.C: Move physically away from the student and provide support only intermittently rather than constantly during the independent warm-up worksheet.
options.D: Float around the room to circulate among the other students during the independent warm-up worksheet.
